progress_summary: Three rounds of UI work merged. Round 1: 5 mobile-demo direction previews → Direction A picked → production MobileWorkbench. Round 2: mobile architecture diagram (vertical stack, no JS, dark default). Round 3: replaced page-summary + OnboardingTour with one-step WelcomePopup, full-bleed workbench, top-right feedback pill, skill detail TOC restyle (fade-in-bg → cream on region hover → ember on link hover → click-to-flash heading bump + 10s ember→cream fade).

progress_table.label: Move desktop feedback pill to top-right

key_decisions: Direction A (Bottom-Tab IDE) selected from 5 previews — preserves IDE metaphor, learnable, all info reachable.
Mobile architecture diagram: static, no JS, dark default, vertical stack of <details> bands. Replaces interactive desktop diagram below 767px.
WelcomePopup: one-step modal with DNS checkbox (localStorage `opchain-demo-welcome-seen`). Auto-opens on every page load unless DNS set. Skipped on mobile.
Desktop full-bleed workbench: dropped page-summary card so the IDE owns the page (78vh height contract retained).
Feedback pill desktop: top-right (top:1rem right:1rem z-index:100). Mobile unchanged — Header.astro already houses the launcher there.

Skill TOC default state uses var(--subtle) (~4.85:1 on obsidian, AA pass) so it blends into bg without failing contrast. Region hover brightens to var(--text); per-link hover overrides to var(--accent). Active-in-viewport entry stays ember.
Click-to-flash uses 320ms scale+nudge bump and 10s linear color fade ember→cream. prefers-reduced-motion disables both.
user_preferences: Wants HTML previews to evaluate before approving direction (round 1).
Comfortable delegating defaults when no preference ("go ahead and implement option A fully").
Likes brand-on voice and full-bleed/immersive surfaces over heavy chrome.

next_actions: Manual phone QA on staging: /demo workbench, /architecture mobile diagram, real-iPhone safe-area check.
Optional: keep B/C/D/E preview directions live at /previews/mobile-demo/ as marketing artifacts (already deployed via #140).
